---
title: "Examples"
toc: false
---

Worked examples ported from the USACE-RMC
[Numerics-Python-Examples](https://github.com/USACE-RMC/Numerics-Python-Examples)
repository (0BSD licensed). Each example exists in a Python version (`bestfitpy`,
a Jupyter notebook you can download and run) and an R version (`bestfitr`, a
Quarto document). The pair uses the same seeds and prints the same numbers: both
packages run the same compiled core with a bit-exact Mersenne Twister.

Every example ends with a reproduction check comparing this port's results
against the outputs embedded in the upstream notebooks, run by the original C#
library. Where a result is seeded and deterministic the match is exact; where an
example was recast onto this package's API the check is statistical and says so.
The [coverage page](coverage.qmd) lists every upstream example and its status
here.

| # | Example | Python | R |
|---|---------|--------|---|
| 00 | Getting started | [Python](00-getting-started/python.ipynb) | [R](00-getting-started/r.qmd) |

More examples land as they are ported; see the coverage page for the full plan.
